The Friday Note — Issue #4

The Pattern Emerges

March 7, 2026 • The Future of PMM
Welcome to Week Four

This week I did something I've never done before: I read every AI + PMM story published in a month. 35 editions. 180 articles. And the pattern that emerged was unmistakable.

The gap between AI-native PMMs and everyone else isn't closing — it's accelerating. We're watching a step function, not a gradual curve. And most of us are on the wrong side of it.

Also in this issue: Evertune, the Trade Desk vets building the Bloomberg Terminal for AI search. Plus a first look at our new Competitive Intelligence Dashboard — an always-on analyst tracking competitor positioning shifts.

—Chris

Featured

I Read Every AI + PMM Story for a Month

After 35 editions and 180 articles, the pattern is unmistakable. Here's what's actually coming — and why most PMMs aren't ready.

The Four Signals:

  • Buyers are becoming agents — 60% of enterprises now have agents in production (G2)
  • Thought leadership is dead — The receipts-based model is replacing the opinions-based model
  • Only 6% of marketers are AI-ready — The gap is a step function, not gradual
  • GEO is the new SEO — If agents can't find you, you don't exist
Read the full analysis →
Vendor of the Week
⭐ Vendor of the Week

Brian Stempeck

CEO & Co-Founder · Evertune

Building the Bloomberg Terminal for AI Search. Stempeck spent 11 years scaling The Trade Desk to $50B. Now he's betting GEO — Generative Engine Optimization — will become the most important channel no PMM is thinking about yet.

The pitch: When 90% of B2B buyers use GenAI to research vendors, traditional SEO isn't enough. Evertune tracks how your brand appears across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Perplexity — and helps you improve it.

📖 Read the Full Profile →

New Tool

🔐 For SAP PMM Team

Competitive Intelligence Dashboard

What if you had an analyst who never slept — monitoring competitor websites for positioning changes, pricing updates, and AI strategy shifts?

We built exactly that. The dashboard tracks:

  • Enterprise Apps: Salesforce, Workday, Oracle, Adobe
  • CDPs: Hightouch, Segment, Amperity, RudderStack
  • Signals: AI Agent Maturity, Positioning Shifts, Pricing Changes, M&A

Key Findings So Far:

  • Salesforce rebranded Data Cloud → "Data 360"
  • Hightouch named Gartner CDP Leader
  • Census acquired by Fivetran (consolidation signal)

Updates weekly: Wed = Enterprise Apps, Thu = CDPs

🔐 Access code: sappmm2026

Explore the Dashboard → · How We Built It →

News Roundup

This Week in AI + PMM

🔥 OpenAI Lands Pentagon Deal — Hours After Anthropic Blacklisted

OpenAI secured a DoD contract for classified networks just hours after Anthropic was designated a "supply chain risk to national security." The divergence: Anthropic refused to remove safety guardrails; OpenAI negotiated.

PMM takeaway: Enterprise AI procurement just got political. Know your vendor's position.

📈 NVIDIA: Enterprise Agentic AI Has Hit an "Inflection Point"

Jensen Huang declared it during earnings: agentic AI adoption has crossed from pilot to production. The numbers back it up — fiscal 2026 revenue reached $216 billion, up 65% YoY.

PMM takeaway: If you're treating AI as experimental, you're already behind.

💰 Enterprise AI-Native Spend Up 94% YoY

New Tropic data from $18B in software spend: AI-Native tools grew 94% while "Primarily SaaS" limped along at 8%. Companies are placing serious bets on purpose-built AI.

PMM takeaway: The budget is flowing to AI-native solutions. Is your positioning AI-native or AI-bolted?

⚠️ The "AI Tax" Is Real: 20-37% Price Increases at Renewal

Same Tropic report: vendors are using AI to push renewal increases of 20-37% — far beyond typical 3-9% uplifts. Tactics include forced SKU migrations and credit-based pricing.

PMM takeaway: Prepare for the pricing conversation. Customers are noticing.

📊 Only 6% of Marketers Have Fully Implemented AI

Reality check from Supermetrics: despite the hype, just 6% of marketers have fully implemented AI. The bigger problem? 52% say external teams define their data strategy.

PMM takeaway: AI adoption isn't a tech problem — it's a data governance problem.
